headline: Presenting goal-directedness work at two ICLR 2026 workshops
summary: We will present our work on behavioural and representational evaluation of goal-directedness in LLM agents at the ICLR 2026 Workshop on World Models and at Agents in the Wild: Safety, Security, and Beyond.

headline: Call for papers: LM Playschool Workshop and Challenge
summary: We have announced the call for papers for the LM Playschool Workshop and Challenge. We invite submissions exploring the frontier of language agents that learn, adapt, and improve through situated interaction, with a focus on conversational, collaborative, goal-oriented, and multi-turn environments.

headline: Two new preprints on LLM agents in extra-linguistic tasks
summary: New work on evaluating language model agents (1) with a combination of behavioural and representational analyses of goal-directedness; (2) with a new active probabilistic reasoning task (inspired from cognitive neuroscience) that isolates two core elements of decision-making under uncertainty: sampling and inference.

headline: Paper accepted at EACL 2026
summary: Work with collaborators from Edinburgh on extending information-theoretic models of language production to visually grounded settings was accepted at EACL 2026.

links.label: Paper: "Is Information Density Uniform when Utterances are Grounded on Perception and Discourse?"

headline: Grants and SPAR mentorship supporting agent-evaluation work
summary: A Cohere Labs Catalyst Grant, a Cosmos Grant, and a SPAR cohort are supporting new work on modelling, measuring, and intervening on goal-directedness and emergent self-interest in LLM agents.

headline: Joining UCL
summary: Joined UCL as an Associate Professor of Computational Linguistics.

headline: ACL and ICML results
summary: Four papers accepted at ACL and two at ICML, including an ICML Spotlight on converting token-level language models to character-level ones.
